panel_question: 消费者知情权与餐饮行业透明度底线

summary.headline: Panel converged on supporting mandatory menu labeling of pre-processed food tiers in restaurants, anchored in existing national policy and international precedent.

key_findings.finding: 明厨亮灶 (open kitchens) provide selective transparency - they show the final preparation step but conceal central kitchen pre-processing, frozen ingredient storage, and factory-made sauce packets.
key_findings.evidence_basis: Cross-verified by SEAT-2 (professional blogger), SEAT-3 (former employee), SEAT-5 (20-year chef). Consistent with CLAIM_GATE C006 (VERIFIED), C016 (CONTRADICTED).

key_findings.finding: National policy already requires restaurants to disclose pre-made food usage. The 六部委 2024-03-21 notification explicitly states '大力推广餐饮环节使用预制菜明示，保障消费者的知情权和选择权'.
key_findings.evidence_basis: SEAT-2 cited the specific policy document. Consistent with CLAIM_GATE C012, C030 (both VERIFIED).

key_findings.finding: A three-tier menu labeling scheme ('made fresh' / 'central-kitchen pre-processed' / 'pre-made heated') is operationally feasible, has international precedent (Japan), and gained support from all active participants.
key_findings.evidence_basis: Proposed by SEAT-2, supported by SEAT-1, SEAT-3, SEAT-4, SEAT-5. SEAT-5 cited Japanese restaurant labeling practices.

key_findings.finding: Consumer position evolved significantly when presented with multi-source information. SEAT-1 shifted from 'experience is enough' to 'support labeling, question integrity' across 6 rounds.
key_findings.evidence_basis: Observable in transcript - SEAT-1's stance changed substantively in rounds 3-4 after exposure to former employee testimony and national policy information.

key_findings.finding: Employee compensation claims (赔偿金分期未付完) conflict with public commitments by management, but remain single-source and unverified.
key_findings.evidence_basis: SEAT-3's testimony only. References搜狐 2026-03 report. Consistent with CLAIM_GATE C021 (UNVERIFIED).

key_findings.finding: Consumer right-to-know has class dimensions - those spending 100+ RMB per meal can afford to discuss information rights, while daily meal budget of 20-30 RMB workers have no meaningful choice.
key_findings.evidence_basis: SEAT-6's consistent indifference and 'not that I don't want to choose, but I have no choice' statement. Elevated by SEAT-4 into a structural observation.

gained_traction: Menu labeling proposal - shifted from radical idea to consensus position
明厨亮灶 limitations - transformed from minority critique to accepted fact
Management integrity concerns - moved from implicit to explicit through employee testimony
failed_to_gain_traction: Complete boycott or rejection of西贝 - explicitly rejected by multiple participants
锅气香精 as widespread practice - acknowledged as existing but not proven as widespread
Equating all chain restaurants - participants maintained distinction between brands

content_distortion_resistance.assessment: The discussion content showed strong resistance to distortion because: (1) Three independent witness types (blogger, employee, chef) provided converging testimony. (2) National policy citation anchored claims in verifiable law. (3) The proposed solution was concrete and moderate. (4) One participant (SEAT-4) consistently played a bridge role preventing polarization. (5) One participant (SEAT-6) provided a natural brake as a completely disinterested party.
vulnerability_points: SEAT-3's unverified compensation and training script claims could amplify without fact-checking
锅气香精 existence could be generalized to widespread usage without evidence
The consensus, while constructive, lacks representation from restaurant industry perspective

real_world_risk.rationale: The consensus is constructive, policy-aligned, and incremental. It does not call for boycotts or extreme action. The labeling proposal is already supported by national policy. The main risk is that unverified claims (compensation issues, training scripts) could spread beyond the panel without proper fact-checking.

recommendations: Fact-check SEAT-3's compensation and training script claims against available evidence
Distinguish between锅气香精 existence (VERIFIED) and specific restaurant usage (UNVERIFIED)
Note that the panel lacked restaurant industry representation, which would have added balance
The three-tier labeling proposal deserves serious consideration as it is moderate, feasible, and policy-aligned
